resources.string: Capture your device\'s posture — the apps you have installed (versions, installer, granted permissions and appop modes) plus a small set of benign, non-secret settings. This is a complement to your encrypted backups: it records what your device looked like so you can rebuild it deliberately after a reset. It reads only; it never changes anything on its own.
Read your installed-app inventory + granted permissions
Read and, per-key with your opt-in, restore benign settings
Without elevation, use your regular backups and the system\'s own backup/transfer tools; device-posture capture needs a read-only privileged shell.
Runs read-only shell reads (pm list packages, dumpsys package, settings get) and saves a JSON posture file in this app\'s private storage. No system apps are modified; nothing is uploaded.
Capture ran but the snapshot could not be written to storage.
That snapshot file could not be read — it may be corrupt.
No snapshots yet. Capture one above.
Removes the posture file captured %1$s from this device. Your apps and settings are untouched.

Apps in this snapshot that are not installed right now. This is a guided list — nothing is reinstalled automatically. Open each in the store to reinstall it yourself. Suite apps and core system apps are excluded.
Every app in this snapshot is already installed.

A small, safe set of settings can be written back, one at a time, only if you opt in. The current value is shown against the captured value first. Non-secret keys only; any key that fails to write is reported honestly.
No restorable settings were captured in this snapshot.
